raw_consumer_statement: This complaint concerns Citibank, N.A.s handling of a credit card billing dispute under the Fair Credit Billing Act, not a complaint against the merchant. Please route to Citibank In XX/XX/year>, I contracted with XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX based on specific verbal representations regarding pricing, services, and staffing. I paid a {$1500.00} deposit on XX/XX/year>. On the scheduled move date, XXXX XXXX XXXX did not appear. Instead, an undisclosed subcontractor, XXXX XXXX XXXX, arrived without prior notice or consent. I was then presented with materially inflated charges that contradicted the agreed estimate and was explicitly threatened that my household goods would not be loaded or delivered unless I paid immediately. Under coercive conditions, without a reasonable ability to refuse or seek alternatives, I paid an additional {$1300.00} on XX/XX/year>. Authorization obtained under misrepresentation and coercion is not valid authorization. I timely disputed both charges with Citibank and provided documentation demonstrating bait-and-switch pricing, undisclosed subcontracting, and coercion at the point of payment. Citibank failed to conduct a reasonable investigation as required under Regulation Z and the Fair Credit Billing Act, relied solely on merchant assertions, ignored the evidence submitted, mischaracterized the dispute as services received, and later denied the dispute on the basis of age only after mishandling the dispute process. Citibank failed to address material misrepresentation, coercion, or invalid authorization and improperly denied the dispute.
